channel.title: Operating on RadioSC
channel.description: Recent content in Operating on RadioSC

item.title: Multimode Operating Practice
item.description:
5 Step Operating Practice

Pausing between transmissions is “key” to reliable multimode digital voice communications on the multimode systems, particularly systems with repeaters like RadioSC1

QSOs that adhere to the following practice will make QSOs better for all operators and enhance communication.

  1. After the transmitting Operator completes a transmission, pause 2 seconds.
  2. After the 2 second pause, PTT and continue pressing PTT for 2 seconds before speaking. This allows the other digital modes and the transcoder to “engage.”
  3. After completing your transmission say “over” or “back to you,” and if 3 or more operators in the QSO, the callsign to which you are passing the QSO in order to mitigate doubling.
  4. Before unkeying continue pressing the PTT for an additonal 2 seconds to allow your transmission and the transcoding process to complete on the other digital modes.
  5. Release PTT.

Other helpful operating hints

  • Be aware of what your display is telling you. If an operator tries to break in a name or call might be seen momentarily on your display even if no voice is heard.

item.title: About
item.description:
RadioSC Composition

Reflector

The Hub of the RadioSC system is our Universal Reflector, URF517. URF517 uses the 2urfd Reflector software.

URF517 has trancoders on Module/Channel A allowing D-STAR, YSF, DMR, and M17 to have QSOs (P25 and NXDN could also be activated if needed). DMR and YSF can commuicate on other channels since they use the same CODEC (so a hardware transcoder is not required).

IcomGateway

Our first D-STAR repeater located at Awendaw, SC also connects to the reflector via the IcomGateway software.
